statement: Before running a full-scale Temporal-backed pilot loop, judge-gym needs an automated end-to-end smoke test (window + run) plus a non-UI worker polling check (task-queue pollers/reachability) so the agent can distinguish infra/polling failures from workflow/LLM failures quickly.
rationale: Current setup docs describe a manual smoke test and the most common Temporal failure mode during pilot operations is 'no workers polling task queue'. Automating both closes a large operator/agent blind spot and reduces iteration time.
keyAssumptions: Temporal CLI or an equivalent programmatic API is available to the agent or CI environment.
The smoke test can run against a dev Convex deployment and the Railway Temporal cluster without requiring local Temporal.
validationCriteria: A script can create a small window and observe evidence + stage progress without human UI inspection.
A script can report whether any workers are polling judge-gym task queues and fail fast if not.
critiquePoints: Alternative is to implement worker heartbeats mirrored into Convex; this could avoid CLI but risks Convex becoming the liveness oracle again.
questions: Do we want the polling check implemented via Temporal CLI, Temporal SDK calls, or a small Railway-side 'health' service?
